title: All-Butter Shortcrust Pastry

overview.description: A crisp, buttery shortcrust pastry suitable for both savoury pies and sweet tarts.

method: Mix the flour and salt together in a large bowl.
Rub the cold butter into the flour until the mixture resembles coarse breadcrumbs, leaving a few pea-sized pieces of butter.
Add the cold water a tablespoon at a time, mixing gently with a knife until the dough just comes together.
Bring the dough together with your hands without kneading.
Flatten into a disc, wrap and chill for at least 30 minutes.
Roll from the centre outwards, turning the pastry a quarter turn every few rolls.
Lift the pastry with the rolling pin and gently line your tin.
Trim any excess and chill again for 15–20 minutes before baking or filling.

learning.did-you-know: Visible flecks of butter are a good thing—they melt during baking to create a flaky, tender pastry.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How to handle pastry lightly, keep the butter cold and roll evenly without shrinking.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A versatile master recipe that supports pies, tarts and quiches throughout the cookbook.
chapman-tips: Cold ingredients are the secret to crisp pastry.
Stop adding water as soon as the dough comes together.
Don't knead the dough or it will become tough.
Chilling before rolling and again before baking helps prevent shrinkage.
notes.batch-cooking: Wrap well and freeze for up to 3 months. Defrost overnight in the fridge before using.
notes.leftovers: Use pastry offcuts for cheese straws, jam tarts or decorative pie tops.
